skill.name: remotion-render
skill.description: Render React and Remotion component code into MP4 videos through the inference.sh belt CLI.
skill.summary: Render TSX Remotion animations into MP4 videos with configurable video settings.
skill.author: infsh-skills

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command and URL patterns because this skill documents use of the inference.sh belt CLI and links to external documentation. The command and network behavior is expected for a remote Remotion renderer, but it creates meaningful risk because user-supplied TSX and props are sent to an external service. The weak cryptography alerts are false positives caused by ordinary Markdown words and table text, with no evidence of cryptographic code or malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Remote CLI Rendering Sends User Code to External Service
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run belt app commands that submit React and Remotion TSX code to infsh/remotion-render. This is the intended feature, but it means project code, props, and rendered assets may leave the local workspace and depend on an authenticated external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented commands clearly send a code field to the remote infsh/remotion-render app through belt. The behavior is legitimate for the skill, but it is a real data exposure and external execution risk.

medium_findings.title: Agent Tool Permission Allows belt Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill metadata allows Bash(belt *), enabling the agent to execute local belt commands. This is required for rendering, but users should treat generated command inputs as sensitive and verify authentication, workspace content, and billing impact before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration and setup requirement provide direct evidence of permitted external command execution. No malicious command is present, so the confidence reflects operational risk rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External Documentation and Asset URLs
low_findings.description: The skill links to inference.sh, Remotion documentation, and a hosted image. These URLs are consistent with the documented rendering workflow and do not show exfiltration by themselves, but they establish external dependencies.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible in Markdown and align with the skill purpose. I did not find evidence that the links collect secrets or bypass user consent.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography alerts do not correspond to cryptographic implementation. They appear to match ordinary prose or table text in the skill description and input schema, with no hash, cipher, or encryption logic present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found Markdown metadata and a parameter table at the reported lines, not cryptographic code. This makes the blocker classification very likely to be a scanner substring false positive.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Pattern: belt app run
dangerous_patterns.description: Multiple examples invoke belt app run with inline input. The pattern is expected for this skill, but agents should not run unreviewed or sensitive payloads through a remote renderer.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is repeated across the documented examples and passes user-provided code as input. The risk is contextual because the command targets the advertised render service.

content.value_statement: Teams often need repeatable video output from React animation code. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to render Remotion TSX through inference.sh into MP4 files.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to render Remotion component code with the belt CLI.
Documents required inputs such as code, duration, FPS, width, height, and codec.
Shows examples for fade-in text, counters, spring animation, props, and sequences.
Identifies supported Remotion and React imports for renderable TSX components.
Includes Python SDK usage for standard and streaming render progress workflows.
limitations: Requires an installed and authenticated inference.sh belt CLI.
Sends render input to an external inference.sh service.
Does not provide local Remotion build or render implementation files.
The skill file documents usage only and does not validate TSX safety itself.

use_cases.title: Generate Product Motion Assets
use_cases.description: Create short MP4 animations from React components for release notes, social posts, or product demos.

use_cases.title: Render Data-Driven Videos
use_cases.description: Use props and Remotion timelines to produce repeatable videos from structured product or analytics data.

use_cases.title: Automate Video Production
use_cases.description: Run scripted renders through the CLI or Python SDK when teams need consistent video output in a workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Render a Simple Animation
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a short Remotion render.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a simple Remotion TSX animation and render it as a three second MP4 at 1920 by 1080.

prompt_templates.title: Render With Custom Settings
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs specific video dimensions and timing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Render my Remotion component as a square video at 1080 by 1080, 60 FPS, and five seconds long.

prompt_templates.title: Render With Dynamic Props
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants reusable video content from component props.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use props for the title, subtitle, and brand color, then render the Remotion video with those values.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Sequenced Motion Graphic
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for timeline structure and remote rendering.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Remotion sequence with three timed sections, spring transitions, and a final callout, then render the MP4 through belt.

output_examples.input: Render a two second fade-in title animation.
output_examples.output: A rendered MP4 with centered text that fades in over a dark background, using the requested duration and frame rate.

output_examples.input: Create a square animated counter for a progress update.
output_examples.output: A 1080 by 1080 MP4 where the number increases over the full timeline and ends at the requested value.

output_examples.input: Render a Remotion component with title and subtitle props.
output_examples.output: A video file generated from the component with the provided text values applied during rendering.

best_practices: Review TSX and props before sending them to the remote renderer.
Keep render duration, resolution, and FPS as small as practical during iteration.
Use props for repeated content so the same component can render many variants.
anti_patterns: Do not render private source code, secrets, or customer data through an external service without approval.
Do not run generated belt commands before checking the input payload and target app.
Do not assume the skill performs local rendering or sandbox validation.

faq.question: What does this skill render?
faq.answer: It renders React and Remotion component code into MP4 video through the inference.sh remotion-render app.

faq.question: Does it require a local Remotion project?
faq.answer: No local project is documented. The examples send TSX code directly to the remote render app.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it use component props?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents a props object that is passed to the exported component.

faq.question: What are the main security considerations?
faq.answer: It executes belt commands and sends render inputs to an external service. Review payloads before running renders.

faq.question: Does it support streaming progress?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes a Python SDK example that streams progress and prints the final video URL.
